acceptance_criteria.statement: Issue #623 must remain bounded to the local mutation-time pre-tool surfaces only, must not reopen issue #617 startup/helper ownership or issue #621 root backlog/commit-parity ownership, and must later return bounded-write evidence proving the implementation diff stayed inside the owned surfaces and approved co-staging.

Issue #623 must keep issues #607, #612, and #614 explicit as dependency or follow-on lanes and must preserve issues #617 and #621 as already-closed sibling boundaries instead of absorbing their owned contracts or enforcement surfaces.
No mutation-path implementation may begin until the issue-local planning packet validates and alternate-family review is recorded.

Future issue-623 implementation proof must require a per-surface proof matrix with concrete blocked and allowed local-path transcripts, including Edit or MultiEdit, named Bash mutation verbs, removed fail-open behavior, one compliant governance-surface allow case, and one read-only or out-of-scope allow case, each with exact command, tool path, exit code, and block or allow result, rather than prose-only attestation.

blocked_on: Issue #617 remains the already-closed startup/helper child and must not be reopened here.
Issue #621 remains the already-closed root backlog/commit-parity child and must not be reopened here.
Issue #612 remains the degraded-fallback schema and CI enforcement lane.
Issue #614 remains the downstream hldpro-sim verifier or drift-gate lane.
Implementation is blocked until the issue-local planning packet validates and alternate-family review is recorded.
notes: Planning-only handoff for issue #623 bootstrap. This child is narrowed to local mutation-time pre-tool fail-closed hardening only. It must validate its issue-local packet and record alternate-family review before any implementation is promoted. Future implementation closure must include both a per-surface proof matrix and bounded-write evidence.
